+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: e080
+project: spatial-nav
+title: 'Test harness: vitest-browser integration for spatial nav (React → Tauri → Rust → DOM)'
+---
+## What
+
+Every spatial nav "fix" this session was done blind — no failing test, no reproducible scenario, just live code edits watched against a running dev app. That is exactly why grid nav now regresses every second change. We need a TDD harness sufficient to drive a real React tree, invoke `spatial_navigate` against a real Rust state machine, and assert against the DOM.
+
+The existing test landscape:
+- `vitest` unit tests — mock `invoke`, never exercise Rust. Useless for spatial.
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-integration.browser.test.tsx` — jsdom browser-mode, also mocks `invoke`.
+- `cargo test -p kanban-app` spatial integration tests — drive Rust directly, no React/DOM.
+- `kanban-app/e2e/spatial-nav.e2e.ts` (WebdriverIO) — exists but never successfully run; also tracked in `01KPGXA68N16B5VMBBT285AD5Z`.
+
+None of these cover the loop we actually need to test: **click DOM element → setFocus in React → spatial_focus to Rust → press key → spatial_navigate in Rust → focus-changed event → claim callback → data-focused flips on the new DOM element**.
+
+### Failing test (goal)
+
+```tsx
+it("grid: pressing 'j' from cell (0,0) moves focus to cell (1,0)", async () => {
+  await renderWithFixture3x3Board();
+  const cell00 = screen.getByTestId("data-moniker:field:task:task-1-1.title");
+  await userEvent.click(cell00);
+  await waitFor(() => expect(cell00).toHaveAttribute("data-focused", "true"));
+  await userEvent.keyboard("j");
+  const cell10 = screen.getByTestId("data-moniker:field:task:task-1-2.title");
+  await waitFor(() => expect(cell10).toHaveAttribute("data-focused", "true"));
+});
+```
+
+Variants to write next (sibling tasks depend on these):
+- `h` from col 1 → row selector; `l` from row selector → col 1
+- `k` from top row of grid → active perspective tab
+- `h` from leftmost column (past row selector) → active LeftNav button
+
+### Approach — two viable options
+
+**Option A. vitest-browser mode** (what the project already uses for `board-integration.browser.test.tsx`): real browser, jsdom for DOM, but `invoke` is mocked. Would need to replace the `invoke` mock with a real in-process `SpatialState` (the state machine is `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav`). A thin JS adapter calls the same `SpatialState` via neon/wasm, OR we inline a TS clone of the state machine for tests only. Both are ugly.
+
+**Option B. tauri-driver + WebdriverIO** — real app, real IPC. Already scaffolded at `kanban-app/e2e/`. The canonical choice for this kind of thing. The blocker task `01KPGXA68N16B5VMBBT285AD5Z` covers getting it to run once.
+
+Prefer B. Reuse the 3×3 fixture factory (`kanban-app/src/test_support.rs::write_3x3_board`) and the existing `--only` flag. Add a tags grid fixture + inspector fixture alongside.
+
+### Subtasks
+
+- [ ] Confirm tauri-driver runs locally (depends on `01KPGXA68N16B5VMBBT285AD5Z`)
+- [ ] Extend `kanban-app/src/test_support.rs` with a `write_grid_of_tags(n_tags)` fixture
+- [ ] Add `kanban-app/e2e/spatial-nav-grid.e2e.ts` — the "cell j → cell below" scenario; MUST fail against today's broken grid
+- [ ] Add `kanban-app/e2e/spatial-nav-inspector.e2e.ts` — "field k/j moves between fields"
+- [ ] CI integration confirmed (depends on `01KPG80F9WKGCKCGCFZ7PQTD09`)
+
+### Acceptance
+
+- [ ] A failing E2E exists for grid nav
+- [ ] A failing E2E exists for any scenario introduced by sibling tasks BEFORE its fix lands
+- [ ] Subsequent spatial-nav fixes each start with a failing E2E scenario and ship with it passing
\ No newline at end of file

+Every spatial nav "fix" this session was done blind — no failing test, no reproducible scenario, just live code edits watched against a running dev app. That is exactly why grid nav regressed every second change. We need a TDD harness sufficient to drive a real React tree, invoke the spatial commands against a deterministic Rust state machine, and assert against the DOM — **using vitest only, no WebDriver / tauri-driver / external process.**
+
+### Why NOT tauri-driver / WebdriverIO
+
+The previous webdriver-based E2E attempt (`kanban-app/e2e/spatial-nav.e2e.ts`, `wdio.conf.ts`, tauri-driver install requirement) failed to run reliably and was removed in commit `89e08625c`. Do not resurrect it. CI and local dev must stay on vitest.
+
+### Approach — vitest-browser with a deterministic invoke mock
+
+Two complementary tiers.
+
+**Tier 1. Rust state-machine tests** (already exist in `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_state.rs` + `swissarmyhammer-spatial-nav/src/spatial_nav.rs`): pure unit tests of the beam-test algorithm, layer stack, focus memory, overrides. These stay as-is and catch regressions in the core algorithm.
+
+**Tier 2. React vitest-browser tests with a `SpatialState` JS shim:** a small TS implementation of the navigation + registry routines that the Rust command handlers would otherwise drive. Route `@tauri-apps/api/core`'s `invoke` through `vi.mock(...)` to the shim. Tests render the full React tree (`AppShell`, `FocusLayer`, `FocusScope`s, the board/grid/inspector components) against this shim so:
+
+- `spatial_register` → shim's registry Map
+- `spatial_focus` → shim's focused_key + emit `focus-changed` synchronously
+- `spatial_navigate` → shim runs the beam test + scoring and emits `focus-changed`
+- `spatial_push_layer` / `spatial_remove_layer` → shim's layer stack
+- `__spatial_dump` → shim returns its internal state

-The existing test landscape:
-- `vitest` unit tests — mock `invoke`, never exercise Rust. Useless for spatial.
-- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/board-integration.browser.test.tsx` — jsdom browser-mode, also mocks `invoke`.
-- `cargo test -p kanban-app` spatial integration tests — drive Rust directly, no React/DOM.
-- `kanban-app/e2e/spatial-nav.e2e.ts` (WebdriverIO) — exists but never successfully run; also tracked in `01KPGXA68N16B5VMBBT285AD5Z`.
+The shim MUST be behavior-equivalent to Rust — verified by shared test fixtures (a list of `(before-state, command, after-state)` triples run against both implementations, or a parity test that imports cases from a JSON file built from Rust tests). Specifying identical behavior in two implementations is the trade-off; the payoff is vitest-only CI and fast local iteration.

-None of these cover the loop we actually need to test: **click DOM element → setFocus in React → spatial_focus to Rust → press key → spatial_navigate in Rust → focus-changed event → claim callback → data-focused flips on the new DOM element**.

+- Click handlers actually running through React's event system
+- `focus-changed` event listener wiring, claim callbacks flipping `data-focused`
+- Scope chain walking from the focused FocusScope up through CommandScopeProviders
+- Keybinding resolution picking the right `execute` handler
